title: The Body Keeps The Score
author: Bessel van der Kolk
synopsis: A pioneering researcher transforms our understanding of trauma and offers a bold new paradigm for healing. Dr. van der Kolk uses recent scientific advances to show how trauma literally reshapes both body and brain, compromising sufferers' capacities for pleasure, engagement, self-control, and trust.

title: Welcome to the Hyunam-dong Bookshop
author: Hwang Bo-reum
synopsis: Yeongju is burned out. Quitting her job and divorcing her husband, she moves to a quiet residential neighborhood outside Seoul and opens the Hyunam-dong Bookshop. It depicts her journey towards inner peace and business success, while focusing on the other people around her and their personal journeys of self-discovery.

title: All the Lovers in the Night
author: Mieko Kawakami
synopsis: Fuyuko Irie is a freelance copy editor in her mid-thirties. Working and living alone in a city where it is not easy to form new relationships. When she notices her reflection in a storefront window, she sees a spiritless woman and decides to do something about it. A beautiful novel exploring the quiet tragedy of ordinary life.

title: Norwegian Wood
author: Haruki Murakami
synopsis: A stunning and elegiac novel of first love, the story of Toru, a quiet and preternaturally serious young college student in Tokyo, who is devoted to Naoko, a beautiful and introspective young woman, but their mutual passion is marked by the tragic death of their best friend years before.

title: Against the Loveless World
author: Susan Abulhawa
synopsis: As Nahr sits, locked away in solitary confinement, she reflects on the dramatic events that landed her in prison. Born in Kuwait to Palestinian refugees, her family teeters on the brink of poverty, the US invasion of Iraq makes her a refugee, and she eventually lands in Palestine. An absolutely magnificent portrait of struggle, survival, and liberation.

title: The Art of Thinking Clearly
author: Rolf Dobelli
synopsis: Have you ever invested time in something that, with hindsight, just wasn't worth it? Or continued doing something you knew was bad for you? These are examples of cognitive biases, simple errors we all make in our day-to-day thinking. But by knowing what they are and how to spot them, we can avoid them and make better choices. Simple, clear, and always surprising, this indispensable book will change the way you think and transform your decision-making.

title: Minor Detail
author: Adania Shibli
synopsis: Minor Detail begins during the summer of 1949, one year after the war that the Palestinians mourn as the Nakba, and the Israelis celebrate as the War of Independence. Israeli soldiers capture and rape a young Palestinian woman, and kill and bury her in the sand. Many years later, a woman in Ramallah becomes fascinated to the point of obsession with this minor detail of history. A haunting meditation on war, violence and memory.

title: Mornings in Jenin
author: Susan Abulhawa
synopsis: A heart-wrenching, powerfully written novel that does for Palestine what The Kite Runner did for Afghanistan. It is a multi-generational story about a Palestinian family forcibly removed from the olive-farming village of Ein Hod by the newly formed state of Israel in 1948.

title: The Art of Explanation
author: Ros Atkins
synopsis: In this book, Ros shares the secrets he has learned from years of working in high-pressure newsrooms, identifying the ten elements of a good explanation and the seven steps you need to take to express yourself as persuasively and accurately as possible.

title: Wuthering Heights
author: Emily Bronte
synopsis: The tale of the intense love between Heathcliff and Catherine Earnshaw. Catherine, forced to choose between passionate, tortured Heathcliff and gentle, well-bred Edgar Linton, surrenders to the expectations of her class.

title: Pride and Prejudice
author: Jane Austen
synopsis: Set in the Regency era of England, the story follows the Bennet family, particularly the second eldest daughter, Elizabeth, as she navigates the social conventions of the time and the search for a suitable husband.

title: White Nights
author: Fyodor Dostoevsky
synopsis: Set in St. Petersburg, it is the story of a young man fighting his inner restlessness. A light and tender narrative, it delves into the torment and guilt of unrequited love.

title: Good Arguments: How Debate Teaches Us to Listen and Be Heard
author: Bo Seo
synopsis: A champion debater shares his secrets to arguing well and shows how we can improve our relationships, our careers, and our lives through better communication.

title: Letters to Milena
author: Franz Kafka
synopsis: In no other work does Kafka reveal himself as in the Letters to Milena, which begin essentially as a business correspondence but soon develop into a passionate letter love.
